title: Pilibhit Tiger Reserve
tagline: Pilibhit Tiger Reserve — wildlife reserve of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Pilibhit Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve located in Pilibhit district of Uttar Pradesh, which was notified as a tiger reserve in 2014.
overview.description: Pilibhit Tiger Reserve is a tiger reserve located in Pilibhit district of Uttar Pradesh, which was notified as a tiger reserve in 2014. It forms part of the Terai Arc Landscape in the upper Gangetic Plain along the India-Nepal border. The habitat is characterised by sal forests, tall grasslands and swamp maintained by periodic flooding from rivers. The Sharda Sagar Dam extending up to a length of 22 km (14 mi) is on the boundary of the reserve.

topPlaces.name: 1991 Sikh killings in Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: On 12 July 1991, a vehicle carrying 25 Sikh pilgrims was stopped by the police at a checkpoint in Budaun district of Uttar Pradesh, India. Eleven of the male passengers were detained by police, ten of whom were later shot dead by police on 13 July 1991 in three different locations in the deep forest of Pilibhit tiger reserve in Pilibhit distinct, who alleged they were terrorists.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pilibhit Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pilibhit Tiger Reserve is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pilibhit Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Bareilly Junction (76 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pilibhit Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pilibhit Tiger Reserve at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pilibhit Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: Stays in Pilibhit Tiger Reserve start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pilibhit Tiger Reserve?
faq.a: The top attractions are 1991 Sikh killings in Uttar Pradesh, Jamania, Pilibhit.
